acceptanceSignals: Employee-visible vendor list is always filtered by plant and active service window.
Admin mapping changes take effect immediately and are auditable.
Deliverability checks are enforced server-side for browse/search/order APIs.

reinforcementResults.name: HTTP Contract Sync Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: The current feature satisfies the active HTTP contract policy: formal vendor-plant delivery mapping endpoints are declared in OpenAPI, runtime route-to-spec parity is enforced by test/CI, generated TypeScript client artifacts are present with drift gating, and error-code/error-schema mappings for delivery-mapping endpoints are explicitly test-verified.

reinforcementResults.name: No Legacy Path Rule
reinforcementResults.summary: Static review indicates the feature meets the active policy: vendor-plant-time mappings are enforced server-side in the delivery policy and HTTP execution gateway for browse/search/order paths, Asia/Taipei fixed business-time semantics are implemented, deterministic precedence for overlaps is defined (precedence -> revision -> mapping id), admin upsert/delete mutations are immediately effective with audit entries, and no legacy/fallback compatibility branches for the superseded deliverability model were found in current routes/DTOs/tests.

excludedApproaches: Internal UTC-only business boundary logic for cutoff/availability.
executionConstraints: Evaluate service windows with fixed Asia/Taipei business-time semantics.
Store and enforce vendor-plant-time mappings as authoritative backend constraints.
Define deterministic precedence for overlapping mapping rules.
name: Vendor Plant Delivery Mapping
rationale: Incorrect mapping causes non-deliverable exposure and operational failure.
summary: Control where and when each vendor can serve and enforce those rules globally.
